verses.commentary: Timing matters. Jesus protects His life until the appointed hour.
The Festival of Tabernacles was near. Sukkot, celebrating wilderness wandering and God's provision. Jesus will fulfill its symbolism.
They want public validation. Fame-seeking advice.
Political calculation. They think like unbelievers.
Family rejection. Closest relationships miss His identity. Familiarity breeds unbelief.
My time is not yet here; for you any time will do. Divine timetable versus human urgency. Jesus operates on the Father's schedule, not social pressure.
Jesus' righteousness exposes sin. Truth provokes hatred.
I am not going up to this festival, because My time has not yet fully come. Not yet means timing, not refusal. His hour governs all decisions.
Delay is not denial.
He goes on His terms, His way. Sovereignty in movement.
The Jewish leaders were watching for Jesus and asking, Where is He? Hostile surveillance. They seek to arrest, not worship.
Division over Jesus' identity. Some say good man, others say deceiver. No neutral ground. Whispers reveal conflict.
Political intimidation controls speech. Courage required to confess Christ.
Midpoint arrival. Public teaching in official space.
Unauthorized wisdom. They question His education, not His truth.
My teaching is not My own. It comes from the One who sent Me. Divine origin. Jesus does not invent but reveals. Authority derived from the Father.
Willing obedience produces recognition. Doing precedes full knowing.
Jesus seeks the Father's honor alone.
They claim Moses but murder the Messiah. Law-keepers are law-breakers.
You are demon-possessed. Who is trying to kill You? The crowd denies what leaders plot. Ignorance or deflection. They dismiss His claim as paranoia.
I did one miracle, and you are all amazed. Reference to John 5, healing on Sabbath. One sign still reverberates. Controversy lingers.
Yet, because Moses gave you circumcision, you circumcise a boy on the Sabbath. Rabbinic logic. Circumcision on the eighth day, even if Sabbath. Law permits Sabbath work for covenant sign.
Partial restoration permitted; why not total? Their logic is inconsistent.
Look deeper. Evaluate rightly.
Everyone knows the plot. Yet He teaches publicly.
Why no arrest? Does silence mean acceptance?
They think Messiah's origin will be mysterious. Ironically, they don't know Jesus' true origin.

Yes, you know Me, and you know where I am from. I am not here on My own authority, but He who sent Me is true. You do not know Him. Ironic agreement. They know Nazareth, miss heaven. True origin unknown.
Jesus comes from the Father's presence. Mission and origin unified.
Timing trumps human intention. They cannot touch Him until appointed time.
Logic: greater works unlikely. Jesus must be Messiah.
Leaders act to stop the movement.
I am with you for only a short time, and then I am going to the One who sent Me. Limited opportunity. His presence is temporary. Return to the Father imminent.
They will seek too late. Heaven is inaccessible to unbelievers.

The Jews said to one another, Where does this man intend to go that we cannot find Him? Will He go where our people live scattered among the Greeks? Ironic prophecy. Jesus will go to Gentiles through the church, not physically. They speak truth unknowingly.
Spiritual truth sounds like riddle. Heaven beyond their grasp.
Water ceremony on Tabernacles. Jesus offers Himself as true water. Universal invitation.
Not just receiving but overflowing. Believers become sources. Isaiah 58:11, Zechariah 14:8 echoed.
The Spirit awaits glorification. Pentecost depends on resurrection and ascension.
Prophet like Moses. Recognition dawns.
Davidic origin expected from Bethlehem. They don't know He was born there.
Correct theology, wrong conclusion. They don't investigate His actual birthplace.
Jesus forces decision. No neutrality possible.
They desire but cannot execute. God's sovereignty protects.
Guards return empty-handed. Leaders demand explanation.
No one ever spoke the way this man does. Unprecedented authority. Guards overwhelmed by His words. Truth captivates even opponents.
You mean He has deceived you also? Leaders scoff. Gullibility charged. They interpret conviction as deception.
Elite consensus against Jesus. They assume education guarantees truth.
Spiritual elitism. They curse those whom God blesses.
Previously secret seeker now speaks. Character reference: John 3.
Due process required. Fair hearing demanded. Courageous minority voice.

Are you from Galilee, too? Look into it, and you will find that a prophet does not come out of Galilee. Geographic prejudice. They mock Nicodemus. Actually, prophets did come from Galilee: Jonah, Nahum, possibly Hosea.
Council disperses without resolution. Tension unresolved. Next confrontation looms.
